title: Widen Route/TerminalProps for navigation without a full AgentRow (S5)
action: Per S5, define and export ONE canonical type from web/src/main.ts, named NewPaneRef: { pane_id: string; workspace_id: string; label: string; name?: string }. This is the single source of truth for the post-create navigation reference -- no other cell may re-derive or restate its field set (fixes a plan-review BLOCKER: three cells previously described this shape inconsistently). Widen main.ts's Route so its 'terminal' variant accepts either the existing AgentRow or a NewPaneRef (a second Route variant, or a union on the existing one -- implementer's choice). Widen terminal.ts's TerminalProps.agent to accept AgentRow | NewPaneRef. The existing AgentRow-based path (opening terminal detail from the switcher's agent list) must stay byte-for-byte unchanged -- this is additive, not a replacement (per parent D6). terminal.ts currently reads agent.kind, agent.display, and agent.pane_id; for a NewPaneRef these must render sensibly (e.g. 'shell' as the kind when name is absent, the preset-derived name when present) rather than showing 'undefined' or throwing -- extract this derivation (NewPaneRef/AgentRow -> {kind, display}) as a small pure exported function and unit-test it directly. Do not make the primary proof of correctness a full renderTerminal() DOM invocation: xterm's Terminal.open() touches HTMLCanvasElement.getContext, which jsdom does not implement -- this repo's own full verify run already shows jsdom logs that as a non-fatal warning rather than throwing, so a DOM smoke test may still be added, but the pure-function test is the reliable, required proof.

truths: Opening terminal detail via the existing switcher agent-card flow (a full AgentRow) is byte-for-byte unchanged
The pure kind/display derivation function returns a sane, non-'undefined' result for both an AgentRow and a NewPaneRef (with and without name set)
NewPaneRef always requires pane_id, workspace_id, and label; name is the only optional field

artifacts.substantive: exports NewPaneRef {pane_id, workspace_id, label, name?}; Route's terminal variant accepts both AgentRow and NewPaneRef
TerminalProps accepts AgentRow | NewPaneRef; a pure exported function derives {kind, display} from either

key_links: main.ts's navigate() still calls renderTerminal for both Route shapes
prohibitions: Do not change how an existing AgentRow-based navigation behaves or looks
Do not touch web/src/views/switcher.ts or web/src/api.ts in this cell

verify: cd web && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run test/terminal.test.ts

trace.outcome: Added canonical NewPaneRef type in main.ts and widened Route + TerminalProps to accept AgentRow | NewPaneRef; extracted pure terminalHead() deriving {kind,display} for either shape, unit-tested directly. AgentRow render path byte-for-byte unchanged.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": ["web/test/terminal.test.ts (existing stripAnsiLen suite, still green)"],
  "tests_added_or_changed": ["web/test/terminal.test.ts: new terminalHead suite, 3 cases — AgentRow passthrough, NewPaneRef without name (kind 'shell'), NewPaneRef with name (kind = preset name)"],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Before this change TerminalProps.agent was AgentRow-only and the header read props.agent.kind / props.agent.display directly; the AgentRow-passthrough test asserts terminalHead(agentRow) returns exactly {kind: agent.kind, display: agent.display}, pinning that the existing switcher->terminal path is byte-for-byte unchanged. The NewPaneRef branch is a genuinely new surface (see deliberate_exceptions).",
  "verification_run": "cd web && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run test/terminal.test.ts -> tsc no errors; vitest 5 passed (5), 0 failed. jsdom HTMLCanvasElement.getContext logged to stderr as the anticipated non-fatal warning, not thrown.",
  "deliberate_exceptions": ["NewPaneRef navigation is a new capability with no prior behavior to characterize as a red 'before' — a plain shell structurally cannot produce an AgentRow (S5/P1), so this path could not exist before; correctness is proven by the two new NewPaneRef cases, not a pre-change failure."]
}
trace.verify_output: tsc --noEmit: no errors. vitest: Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 5 passed (5). (jsdom HTMLCanvasElement.getContext logged to stderr as a non-fatal warning, not thrown — as the cell anticipated.)
trace.verify_command: cd web && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run test/terminal.test.ts

checks.id: NewPaneRef exported from main.ts with exactly {pane_id: string, workspace_id: string, label: string, name?: string}
checks.evidence: web/src/main.ts:12-17 export interface NewPaneRef { pane_id: string; workspace_id: string; label: string; name?: string } — four fields, name is the only optional one, exact match

checks.id: Route's terminal variant accepts AgentRow | NewPaneRef
checks.evidence: web/src/main.ts:19-22 Route union: { name: "terminal"; agent: AgentRow | NewPaneRef }
web/src/views/terminal.ts:6-8 TerminalProps { agent: AgentRow | NewPaneRef; ... }; NewPaneRef imported from ../main

checks.id: Existing AgentRow render path behavior byte-for-byte unchanged
checks.evidence: web/src/views/terminal.ts terminalHead() else-branch returns {kind: agent.kind, display: agent.display}; discriminant "workspace_id" in agent is safe because AgentRow (api.ts:7-17) has field workspace, not workspace_id (workspace_id at api.ts:26 belongs to Destination). Template lines now read local kind/display vars but resolve to the identical values for any AgentRow, so rendered output is unchanged; refactor is additive

checks.id: Pure exported function derives {kind, display} from either shape, unit-tested for both incl. NewPaneRef with/without name
checks.evidence: web/src/views/terminal.ts:15-24 export function terminalHead(...): {kind, display}; web/test/terminal.test.ts:18-62 three direct cases — AgentRow passthrough, NewPaneRef no-name (kind 'shell'), NewPaneRef with-name (kind = preset name)

checks.id: Primary proof is the pure-function test, not a full renderTerminal() DOM invocation
checks.evidence: web/test/terminal.test.ts imports only stripAnsiLen and terminalHead; the terminalHead suite calls terminalHead() directly with no renderTerminal()/DOM invocation

checks.id: No literal 'undefined' renders for a nameless NewPaneRef (shell case)
checks.evidence: web/src/views/terminal.ts:21 returns kind: agent.name ?? "shell"; test terminal.test.ts:40-49 asserts terminalHead(no-name ref) === {kind:'shell', display:label}
